name: Back Lock
preceding_figure_names: Progressive Chasse To Right, Tipsy To L
following_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Tipple Chasse to Right, Running Finish, Tipsy To R
notes:
When one is doing Back Lock, the partner is doing Forward Lock.
The Man (but not the Lady) may use foot rise at the end of 1.The Footwork would be THT, but left heel must not rise abruptly.
The Lady must release the toe of the left foot from the floor as it starts to move back to become step 3.
Body should be backing between wall and DW throughout.
Lock step has zero turn, more rotation. Rotate more to R.

name: Closed Telemark
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Quarter Turn to Left, Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Forward Lock, Fish Tail, Four Quick Run, Six Quick Run
notes:
When stepping RF, keep contact with Man, step shouldn't be too close to Man. Need to leave room between Lady's RF and Man's LF for Man to step through his next OP step.
Shape head to right (actually by keeping the head where it is, when body turns left, head will be in the shaping right position). Then, head returns to left at next step.
Lowering at end of the step, before driving into next step.

steps.action: Forward step, turn left, sway, use CBM, rise
comments: Start with LF forward in FDC, begin turn left

steps.action: Back step, turn left, sway, use CBM, slight rise
comments: Strong big step back, use CBM
Do not take shoulders back, do not leave center fwd which will block Man.
R shoulder connect down, R side is anchor for left turning figure. Feel strong upward, leftward through left side (L side slightly raised or heel turns)

name: Cross Chasse
preceding_figure_names: Change of Direction, Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Turn, Reverse Pivot, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Forward Lock, Fish Tail
following_figure_names: Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Forward Lock, Fish Tail
notes:
Styling option: turn head to R

Running Cross Chasse
In stead of closing feet above, keep moving ('running") forward:
Leader
S: RF fwd, CBMP, OP | FDW | HT
S: LF fwd | FDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: RF diagonally fwd | FDW | T | -- | Sway(L) | up
Q: LF fwd, L shoulder leading | FDW | TH | -- | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: LF back, CBMP | BDW | TH
Pay attention not to do a lot rotation through shoulders.
S: RF back | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1, NFR
R shoulder connects down.
Q: LF diagonally back | BDW | T | -- | Sway(R) | up
Do not rotate L shoulder.
Stretch L side.
Q: RF back, R shoulder leading | BDW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 3
S: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --

name: Cross Swivel
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Tipple Chasse To Right, Running Finish, Fish Tail, Tipsy To R
notes: Alternative styling options include RF to side, knees inward; LF to side, knee inward, a little space is ok. Body forms a line, slanted straight to R, keep left side lifted. Give R side to Man. Stay connected. Turn head to R. Rise in body before next S to lower to normal (not sinking down).

comments: Alternatively for styling, RF to side, knees inward
Use hip joint to start the turn, standing knee (R knee) stays forward over the standing foot (RF), do not move the knee off the standing foot.
Use ab muscle to pull to the back of foot, do not leave middle fwd and shoulder weight back.
Heel of RF does not lower until the turn is completed.
Head is still left. At end of &: there is "up" rise feeling before the next S lower to normal.
Alternatively for styling, LF to side, knee inward, a little space is ok.
Body forms a line, slanted straight to R, keep left side lifted. Give R side to Man. Stay connected.
At end of &: rise in body before next S to lower to normal. (not "sinking down")
Body forms a line, slanted straight to R, keep left side lifted. Give R side to Man. Stay connected.

name: Double Reverse Spin
preceding_figure_names: Quarter Turn to Left (Heel Pivot), Natural Turn With Hesitation, Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Progressive Chasse To Right, Double Reverse Spin, Cross Chasse, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish, Quick Open Reverse, Closed Telemark, Cross Swivel, Tipsy To R (LF fwd, H, S into Tipsy to R), Change of Direction
notes:
The Double Reverse Spin may be started LOD (chart above), DC, or DW. When starting DW, lady: before stepping RF back, turn knee 1/8 to L, step back, so RF is stepping straight down LOD. Body feel quite twisted.
The step 1 of both Double Reverse Spin and Progressive Chasse to Right feel the same, the difference is the next step.
For Double Reverse Spin: Man steps DC, toward Lady's left shoulder (not her sternum).
For Progressive Chasse to Right, Man moves toward LOD, making Lady to step pass her LF to the side.
Double Reverse Spin is a traveling step, first Man goes, then Lady goes. It needs to move down the floor.
The amount of turn may vary between 3/4 and a whole turn.
Complete Turn:
step 1-2: Man 3/8, Lady: 1/2
step 2-3: Man 5/8: Lady: 3/8
step 3-4: lady: Lady: 1/8
3/4 Turn:
step 1-2: Man 1/4 (or 3/8), Lady: 3/8
step 2-3: Man: 1/2 (or 3/8), Lady: 1/4
step 3-4: Lady 1/8

comments: Start to rise at end of 1
Draws trailing foot in slowly, both legs should straighten
Draw center back to spine. Do not leave body to Man.
Turn head slightly more to left (don't do it too early, though)
Do not lower too early, lower on "&". Finish with L side positive/fwd.

name: Fishtail
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Cross Chasse, Cross Swivel, Forward Lock, Four Quick Run, Progressive Chasse, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Six Quick Run, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Forward Lock, Four Quick Run, V6, Tipsy To L, Six Quick Run
notes: The Fishtail may be danced DW with no turn. Fishtail is often dance after Cross Swivel. After LF back (Lady shapes to L, RF can cross in front of LF)

steps.action: fwd in CBMP, OP, start to turn R, Sway(S), CBM, Rise e/o 1
comments: Motion continues down the floor, don't tracktrack.
Man's body does a pendulum swing from left to right.

steps.action: crosses behind RF, Facing LOD, T, 1/8 between 1-2, Sway(R), Up
fwd and slightly to side (small step), Facing DW, T, 1/8 between 2-3, Sway(S), Up
comments: Man lowers while staying on toe at beginning of step, and rises out of it into the forward lock, similar to a tipple chasse.
step 3 is just outside lady's LF

steps.action: diag fwd L side leading, Facing DW, T, --, Sway(S), Up
crosses behind LF, Facing DW, T, --, Sway(S), Up
diag fwd, Facing DW, TH, --, Sway(S), Up. Lower e/o 6
fwd in CBMP, OP, Facing DW, T, --, Sway(S), CBM, --
back in CBMP, Backing DC, T, start to turn R, Sway(S), CBM, Rise e/o 1
comments: Man is stepping outside partner. Watch Man's center, keeping center toward each other.
Take LF cross right behind RF, do not step too big. don't put heel down on this S.
Option - do a big shape/extension (looks like \"dipping\" Lady, but Man should not \"drop\" Lady down)
Keep R side connected with Man, do not break at the hip when doing extension.
After moment of large extension, return to \"normal\" extension to keep balance on next step.
This is more rotation of body, not taking left should down.

steps.action: crosses in front of LF, Backing LOD, T, 1/8 between 1-2, Sway(L), Up
comments: When the lady's RF starts to cross in front of LF on 2, the heel should be in contact with the floor.
RF is almost replace at almost same spot, while body is turning. Pick up center.
There is foot rise, but the knees are flexed.
Do not back track, movement is continuous down on the floor; back to \"normal\" extension, start rotating to R.

steps.action: back and slightly to side (small step), Backing DW, T, 1/8 between 2-3, Sway(S), Up
comments: Keep rotating to R. do not back track
R side is very stretched. Whenever there is rotation it's backed up with an internal sway.
Make sure don't fall back, upper body almost feels more forward in order to move back more powerful. Keep left side lifted. Body has torque, twisted to R.
Hip girdle area has to be free to move, not sticking hip up to try to connect to Man
Head is strongly left, outside of LF.

steps.action: diag back R side leading, Backing DW, T, --, Sway(S), Up
comments: Lock step QQS: don't slow down and lose the power and movement

steps.action: crosses in front of RF, Backing DW, T, --, Sway(S), Up
diag back, Backing DW, TH, --, Sway(S), Up. Lower e/o 6
back in CBMP, Backing DW, T, --, Sway(S), CBM, --

name: Four Quick Run
preceding_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Closed Impetus, Closed Telemark, Fish Tail, Forward Lock, Hover Corte, Natural Spin Turn, Progressive Chasse, Progressive Chasse To Right, Quarter Turn to Right, Quick Open Reverse, Running Finish, Six Quick Run, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Forward Lock, V6 (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into 2-12 V6), Fish Tail, Tipsy To L (into 2-5 Back Lock down LOD), Six Quick Run (LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into Six Quick Run)
notes:
Don't be back-weighted, avoid being steppy, keep smooth motion.
Keep left side strongly to the Man all through the steps.
A series of backward steps - keep body upward and forward toward partner.
Lengthen R side of body, take head strongly left, outside of L elbow.
Do not think about rotating upper body to R, which can take spine off standing leg.

comments: Swivel RF so R knee won't be in Man's way

name: Hover Corte
preceding_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Closed Impetus, Natural Spin Turn, Quarter Turn to Right (Heel Pivot), Quick Open Reverse
following_figure_names: Tipple Chasse to Right, Tipsy to R, Running Finish, Tipsy to L, Four Quick Run, V6, Six Quick Run
notes:
Be aware of Man's center. Drive toward the Man, but not shaping too early. This figure has delayed reaction to Man's body. Man takes head to the foot and then develops the shape out of standing leg.
Connection is lower (hips, upper legs is pressing with Man), allowing even more extension.
Think of the shape coming from lower part of abdominal first, then taking the curve up to upper body.
Don't just bend back from waist and fall back.
The side connects to arm. When giving right ribs/side, the tone extends to R arm and give that toned arm/hand to Man forward.
At beginning: head can still kept left throughout the figure. When more comfortable, head can be more free, open to the right at end of 1st S, then close and turn to L when developing shaping.
After lowering at e/o step 3, hips turn R, makes space to move legs in between. Typically, you breathe out when coming down.

comments: Be aware of Man's center. Drive toward the Man, but not shaping too early. This figure has delayed reaction to Man's body. Man takes head to the foot and then develops the shape out of standing leg.

steps.amount_of_turn: 1/4 to L between 1 and 2. Continue to turn 1/8 on step 2.
comments: To be stable, right shoulder, hip and leg need to be all lined up, weight on RF
R side (ribs) to Man (which will pull hip forward and collect the legs), R hip/middle to Man. Then turn head, stretch/extend.
Connection is lower (hips, upper legs is pressing with Man), allowing even more extension.
Think of the shape coming from lower part of abdominal first, then taking the curve up to upper body.
Don't just bend back from waist and fall back.
The side connects to arm. When giving right ribs/side, the tone extends to R arm and give that toned arm/hand to Man forward.
At beginning: head can still kept left throughout the figure. When more comfortable, head can be more free, open to the right at end of 1st S, then close and turn to L when developing shaping.
After lowering at e/o step 3, hips turn R, makes space to move legs in between. Typically, you breathe out when coming down.

name: Natural Pivot Turn
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Cross Chasse, Fish Tail, Forward Lock, Four Quick Run, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn, Progressive Chasse, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish
following_figure_names: Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Rumba Cross
notes:
Difference between a Pivot Turn and a Spin Turn is that there is no Rise in Pivot Turn. There is Rise (and brush) in Spin Turn.
At corner: turn 3/8 to face DW on new LOD.
Turn amount is up to 1/2 to face BLOD, followed by any Natural figure.

steps.action: RF fwd | FDW | HT | Start to turn R
RF closes to LF | BLOD | TH
LF back (RF held in CBMP) | Point BDW | THT
LF close to RF | FLOD | TH

comments: (Heel is kept in contact with floor)

name: Natural Spin Turn
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Cross Chasse, Fish Tail, Forward Lock Step, Four Quick Run, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn, Progressive Chasse, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish
following_figure_names: Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Four Quick Run, V6 (2-12, heel should lower on previous step), Six Quick Run (heel should lower on previous step), Hover Corte
notes:
The footwork is the same as Waltz Natural Spin Turn. When connecting with V6 (QQS lock step), the footwork for this count becomes THT.
Have continuous swing of the leg through the step.
Leave head behind, turn body. Continue to keep head to left during the whole Natural Spin Turn.
Lower at end of 3.
Rise on toes, it will help to bring the feet together on 3rd step.

comments: Start to rise at end of 1
Have continuous swing of the leg through the step.

steps.action: LF to side and slightly back

comments: Leave head behind, turn body. Continue to keep head to left during the whole Natural Spin Turn.

steps.action: RF diagonally fwd after brushing to LF
comments: When connecting with V6 (QQS lock step), the footwork for this count becomes THT.

preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark,Cross Chasse,Fish Tail,Forward Lock,Four Quick Run,Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left),Natural Pivot Turn,Natural Turn,Progressive Chasse,Running Finish,Running Right Turn,Tipple Chasse To Right,V6,Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish
following_figure_names: Closed Impetus,Tipple Chasse To Right,Running Finish,Tipsy To R

following_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Progressive Chasse To Right, Double Reverse Spin
notes: Sway in step 5-6 is optional.

name: Natural Turn
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Cross Chasse, Fish Tail, Forward Lock, Four Quick Run, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn, Progressive Chasse, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Six Quick Run, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish
following_figure_names: Closed Impetus (after 1-3), Tipple Chasse To Right (after 1-3), Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Cross Chasse (use 6th step of Natural Turn as entry), Forward Lock (and then Quick Open Reverse, after 1-5 of Natural Turn ending DC, ; or and then any Natural figures, after 1-5 of Natural Turn ending DW of new LOD, ), Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish (use 6th step of Natural Turn as entry), Tipsy To R (after 1-4 of Natural Turn), Rumba Cross
notes: The step can be underturned to end Man facing new LOD, in which case, 1/4 turn should be completed between 4-5.

comments: The heel pull can be underturned to face new LOD.

name: Progressive Chasse to Right
preceding_figure_names: Change of Direction, Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Turn With Hesitation, Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Back Lock, Tipple Chasse To Right, Running Finish, Four Quick Run (when preceding figure ends DC or LOD), V6 (6-12, when preceding figure ends DC or LOD), Tipsy To R, Six Quick Run (when preceding figure ends DC)
notes:
Bronze: The Progressive Chassé to R could be started facing LOD and ended backing DW (3/8 turn) when the amounts of turn would be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3 (body turns less), no further turn
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less), slight body turn to L on 3, no further turn.
Silver and Gold: Started facing DC and ended backing LOD (3/8 turn), or started facing DC and ending backing DC (1/2 turn). If 1/2 turn is mad the amounts of turn will be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3, 1/8 between 4-5
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less). Body completes turn on 3, 1/8 between 3-4 (body turns less)

steps.action: RF to side and slightly back
LF to side and slightly fwd

name: Quick Open Reverse
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Forward Lock, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6, Zig-Zag Back Lock And Running Finish
following_figure_names: Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot (after step 3), Four Quick Run, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left) (after 1-3 of Quick Open Reverse), Hover Corte
notes:
When this is the danced after a Forward Lock, one walk on S is added (Man RF fwd, Lady LF back)
Prepping the walk: get to knees, hip rotate to R, which gives more room to move as well.
When the Progressive Chassé is danced after an underturned Natural Spin Turn or an Impetus Turn as an entry to the Quick Open Reverse, the normal alignment of the Progressive Chassé will be (from Man's view):
1. Backing DC against LOD
2. Pointing DC
3,4,5 Facing DC.
When the Progressive Chassé is taken after a Natural Spin turn danced with normal alignment it will be:
1 Backing DC
2 Pointing to LOD
3,4,5 Facing LOD

comments: A Reverse Pivot may be used on step 4.

name: Reverse Pivot
preceding_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn
Closed Impetus
Natural Spin Turn
Quarter Turn to Right
Quick Open Reverse
following_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn
Progressive Chasse To Right
Double Reverse Spin
Cross Chasse
Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish
Quick Open Reverse
Closed Telemark
Cross Swivel
Tipsy To R (after a step fwd on LF)
Change of Direction
notes:
If the Reverse Pivot is started backing DC the alignment for the Pivot (Man) would be "to center, toe turned in".
Note also that this step is in CBMP owing to the body turning strongly to L as the RF foot slips back.

steps.action: Backing LOD in CBMP, small step backwards
comments: Primary contact between man and lady is the inside of the lady's upper thighs: her right thigh to inside of his R leg; her left thigh to outside of his R leg.
Lady's L leg tracks Man's R leg when stepping this step.
LF steps in between Man's legs (Man is stepping his RF back and is turning, which makes room for Lady to step in between his feet), straight forward, do not veer to side.
Make sure keep body and head very left to Man. Keep left side strongly forward (positive), don't inch into Man's space.
Keep the volume in frame, don't make frame smaller. extend arm.
If the Reverse Pivot is started backing DC the alignment for the Pivot (Man) would be "to center, toe turned in".
Note also that this step is in CBMP owing to the body turning strongly to L as the RF foot slips back.

steps.action: Forward LOD in CBMP, small step forward
comments: Make sure keep body and head very left to Man. Keep left side strongly forward (positive), don't inch into Man's space.

name: Rumba Cross
preceding_figure_names: Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn
notes: If preceding the Rumba Cross with 1-5 Natural Turn, a sway to L would be used on step 5 which could be held at the beginning of step 1 of the Rumba Cross. This sway assists in maintaining forward movement through the turn. The Rumba Cross may be danced twice.

steps.amount_of_turn: slight turn to R at end of step

steps.action: to side and slightly back, end with RF fwd in CBMP

name: RunningFinish
preceding_figure_names: Back Lock, Cross Swivel, Hover Corte, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Progressive Chasse To Right
following_figure_names: Forward Lock, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quick Open Reverse, Fish Tail, Four Quick Run, V6, Tipsy To L, Six Quick Run
notes:
Timing can also be: QQSS
Can be commenced in other alignments, e.g. starting BLOD, ending DC.
Man may turn 1/4 between 1-2 when a facing alignment would be used. Lady would turn 1/8 between 1-2 and 1/8 between 2-3.

steps.action: Back in CBMP; Backing DW; Start to turn R; Sway(S); CBM; Rise e/o 1
RF to side and slightly forward; Pointing to LOD; 3/8 between 1-2; body turns less; Sway(L); Up
comments: Man should shape left to allow lady to pass and to make the turn easier.

steps.action: LF forward preparing to step OP left side leading; Facing LOD; Body turn to R; Sway(L); Up, lower e/o 3
RF forward in CBMP, OP; Facing LOD; H; Sway(S); CBM
RF forward in CBMP, OP; Facing DW; HT; Start to turn R; Sway(S); CBM; Rise e/o 1
LF to side; Backing DC; 1/4 between 1-2; Sway(R); Up
RF back, R side leading; Backing LOD; TH; 1/8 between 2-3; Sway(R); Up, lower e/o 3
comments: Lower and push off standing R leg, bigger next step.

steps.action: LF back in CBMP; Backing LOD; T; Sway(S)

name: Running Right Turn
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Fish Tail, Four Quick Run, Progressive Chasse, Rumba Cross, Running Right Turn, V6
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Fish Tail (normal; or when ended DC), Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Forward Lock, Quick Open Reverse (when ended DC), Tipsy To L (Tipsy to L into 2-12 V6 DC or 2-5 Back Lock down LOD)
notes:
Other alignments:
Across corner: 3/8 on Pivot, 1/2 on Natural Turn, 3/8 on Running Finish.
Two corners: 3/8 on Pivot, 3/8 on Natural Turn, 1/4 on Running Finish
Side of room: 1/2 on pivot, 1/2 on Natural Turn, 3/8 on Running Finish
When 3/8 is made on Running Finish, using "pointing" instead of "facing" at step 9 for Man.
Alternative timing SQQ may be used on steps 5-7, in which case, SQQS will be used on the Running Finish.

comments: Across corner: 3/8 on Pivot, 1/2 on Natural Turn, 3/8 on Running Finish.
Two corners: 3/8 on Pivot, 3/8 on Natural Turn, 1/4 on Running Finish
Side of room: 1/2 on pivot, 1/2 on Natural Turn, 3/8 on Running Finish
When 3/8 is made on Running Finish, using "pointing" instead of "facing" at step 9 for Man.
Alternative timing SQQ may be used on steps 5-7, in which case, SQQS will be used on the Running Finish.

name: Six Quick Run
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Closed Telemark, Fish Tail, Forward Lock Step, Four Quick Run, Hover Corte, Natural Spin Turn, Progressive Chasse, Progressive Chasse To Right, Quarter Turn to Right, Running Finish, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6
following_figure_names: Natural Turn (start OP), Fish Tail, Tipsy To L (into 2-12 V6 DC or 2-5 Back Lock down LOD.), Four Quick Run (At corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into Four Quick Run or 2-12 of V6)

comments: Rise e/o preceding step, NFR Up.

name: Tipple Chasse To Right
preceding_figure_names: Back Lock, Cross Swivel, Hover Corte, Natural Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Progressive Chasse To Right, Tipsy To L
following_figure_names: Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quick Open Reverse (at corner, when ended DC), Fish Tail (at corner, when ended DC), Tipsy To R (1-4 at corner, then followed by Tipsy to L), V6 (2-12, at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S into 2-12 V6), Tipsy To L (then into 2-12 V6 DC or 2-5 Back Lock down LOD), Six Quick Run (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S into Six Quick Run)
notes:
The man begins shaping, bending his spine to the right. After the check step, he returns his spine to the left. The degree to which the direction of movement changes determines how much checking is done on the check step. To continue in a line down the floor, there is hardly any check at all.
Silver/Gold: Underturned Tipple Chasse: when starting BLOD after 1-3 of Natural Turn, less turn can be made so this ends with man facing new LOD. Then, step 4 will be to the side, flex knees to check the turn. Continue with 2-5 Forward Lock, moving DC of new LOD. Step 5 as Lady must be taken diagonal back when this alignment is used.
Rise will be: slight rise on 2 and 3, lower on 4, commence to rise e/o 4, continue to rise on 5 and 6, up on 7, lower e/o 7.
footwork: step 4 will be TH, and IE of T of LF
Sway: to R with head turned to R (Lady to L) may be used.
This can be used along the side of the room turning 3/8 on the Chasse to end DC. Follow with Quick Open Reverse or Fishtail.

steps.action: diag fwd preparing to step OP

comments: Shaping to L (using head and body weight) on this tipple (QQ) portion.
Think abut going through Man, stepping LF more toward Man, not going away. Stay connected with Man.
Though this is listed as a "side, slightly back" step...
Compress into the knee without lowering the heel. As Lady compresses into knee, the shape is back to neutral position.
L toe is in contact with floor throughout the next QQ.
Use standing leg to really drive to the next stock step

name: Tipsy to Right
preceding_figure_names: Back Lock, Cross Swivel, Double Reverse Spin, Hover Corte, Natural Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Progressive Chasse To Right, Reverse Pivot, Tipple Chasse To Right, Tipsy To L

comments: Curve, the rotation is mostly in the hip area, follow Man's hip, don't stop turning to R (other you'll block Man), continue the rotation and give Man middle and side and stay connected. Upper body/shoulder should not turn too much which will pull off from partner (but it does turn to R, otherwise Man can't pass). Upper body is diagonally stretched (R side is upward), keep parallel to partner. Push off standing leg to move. Forward with body and arms.

steps.action: RF back, R side leading | Backing DW | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up

steps.action: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | HT | 1/8 between 3-4 | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 4
comments: Rotate hip more and lower more into L knee, push off LF/L leg to drive for bigger movement. Do not go downward in this step. The lowering happens in previous step (weight into heel on previous Q). This step drives forward and up (rise e/o this step). Left side now swings forward (body does not stop - which will make it look like going backward), do not swing too much up, focus on forward, body rotate a lot to the right. Head turns and shapes to R. Do not cross into Man's space. Smooth changes. Movement should feel "free", not constrained. Body breathes.

steps.action: LF fwd L side leading | Curving towards LOD | T | - | Sway(R) | Continue to rise on 5
comments: If Man shapes, Lady turn her head to R, but stay very left, keep ribs/side to Man. Really needs to turn body (rotate to R)

steps.action: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 4-6 | Sway(R) | Up, Lower e/o 6
comments: Do not dip shoulder low, keep shoulders flat, swing forward, traveling. Give center to Man, shape. When swing on Q: do not dip shoulder low, keep should flat, swing forward, traveling. Give center to Man, shape. (But note: If Man shapes, Lady turn her head to R on QQ, keep ribs/side to Man.)

steps.action: LF fwd | Facing LOD | H | - | S | CBM | -
comments: Typically, continue with Feather Finish SQQ. This first LF fwd on S, do not follow Man's curve, step straight forward in between Man's legs, otherwise, at the end of Feather finish, Lady's hip will gap/end up to the side of Man's hip. When swing on Q: do not dip shoulder low, keep should flat, swing forward, traveling. Give center to Man, shape. Breathe, release so body does not stiff up. If Man shapes, Lady turn her head to R on QQ, keep ribs/side to Man.

notes: There are five alignments of the Reverse Wave: Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/8 between 4-6, 3/8 between 7-8 (or less at corner); Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back DW of new LOD, 1/4 between 7-8; Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (1); Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (2); Facing DC 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back the new LOD, 3/8 between 7-8. Alignments 2,4, and 5 are used to turn a corner. Extended Reverse Wave can be added with SQQ steps: Man continues BLOD with TH, LF back; Q: T, RF back, R shoulder leading; Q: TH, LF back in CBMP; Lady continues facing LOD, poise not back weight, shoulder dancing in the back of the feet; then continue from step 4 again. Head can keep head left or shape to R, feeling legs dancing ahead of body.

name: Closed Telemark
preceding_figure_names: Closed Change RF, Closed Wing, Double Reverse Spin, Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot, Hesitation Change, Reverse Pivot, Reverse Turn, Wing
following_figure_names: Closed Wing, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn
notes:
Stay connected with Man.
Do not think about shoulder rotation at all, just think about strong diagonal stretch.
Head: keep head at the same place, rotate body underneath and head ends up on the right.
Keep head to right, then on 1 of next e.g Natural Spin Turn, rotate body underneath (no sharp head movement).

steps.action: fwd, FDC, HT, start to turn L
comments: Use CBM and rise a bit early to lead heel turn.

back, BDC, TH, start to turn L
closes to RF (Heel Turn), LOD, HT
